symbols.name: computeLegacyPayout
symbols.detail: [DEAD] Exported but imported by 0 files | [UNIQUE] No similar functions found in codebase | [NEEDS_FIX] Wild escalation formula omitted: wins containing WILD substitutions return under-calculated payouts. | [LEAN] Straightforward payout computation: resolve leading WILD, count consecutive matches, apply multiplier. No unnecessary abstractions. Note: omits the wild multiplier bonus formula (docs/03-Guides/02-Advanced-Configuration.md) — this is a legacy path, so the simpler formula is intentional. | [NONE] No test file exists. Function has multiple branches (WILD substitution, SCATTER guard, match counting, minimum match threshold) with no coverage. | [UNDOCUMENTED] No JSDoc/TSDoc comment. Missing description of WILD substitution logic, the left-to-right consecutive match requirement, the 3-match minimum, the lineBet derivation (bet/10), and the meaning of the return value (coins awarded).

findings.detail: The function counts WILD symbols toward matchCount (lines 11–14) but never accumulates wildCount separately, so the documented escalation `basePayout × (1 + wildCount) × 2^wildCount` is never applied. Example: WILD+CHERRY+CHERRY (wildCount=1, 3-of-kind CHERRY) should yield `2 × lineBet × 2 × 2 = 8×lineBet`; the current code returns `2×lineBet`. Violates `.anatoly/docs/03-Guides/02-Advanced-Configuration.md` (Wild Multiplier Formula) and `.anatoly/docs/04-API-Reference/03-Types-and-Interfaces.md` (LineWin.payout definition).

actions.description: Track wildCount during the match loop and multiply the final result by (1 + wildCount) × 2^wildCount before returning, matching the documented escalation formula for WILD-inclusive wins.
Remove dead code: `computeLegacyPayout` is exported but unused
Add JSDoc documentation for exported symbol: `computeLegacyPayout`

file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 4.5/10 (1 FAIL, 3 WARN, 13 PASS)

rules.detail: No strict-mode violations visible in the file. tsconfig is external and not evaluated here.
All types are explicit: Symbol[], number. No any usage.
Symbol is an imported string-literal union. String-equality guards are idiomatic and no type assertions are used.
Single-function file with primitive parameters — no utility-type opportunities exist.
lineSymbols is never mutated; the parameter type should be readonly Symbol[] to express that invariant.
No local type definitions; project convention is followed through imported Symbol.
No obvious lint violations: consistent braces, no unused variables, no magic-number issues beyond the domain-noted 10.
computeLegacyPayout is a public export with no JSDoc. At minimum @param and @returns annotations are expected.
Uses Array.prototype.find, nullish coalescing, standard for-loop. No deprecated APIs.
Type import precedes value import. Both are relative project-internal imports. No side-effect or circular imports visible.
Pure synchronous function. No promises, no I/O.

Slot-machine domain inferred from reel/symbol/payout vocabulary and confirmed by .anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/02-Core-Concepts.md and .anatoly/docs/04-API-Reference/03-Types-and-Interfaces.md. bet / 10 uses floating-point division to compute lineBet. In regulated gambling systems payout arithmetic must be exact integer or decimal; IEEE-754 float division (e.g. 1 / 10 = 0.1000000000000000055511...) is not certifiable and can cause cumulative RTP drift. Use integer coin arithmetic throughout and defer formatting to presentation layer.
Single O(n) pass over a short fixed-length array (max 5 reels). No redundant work.
Pure deterministic function with no side effects. Fully injectable via arguments.
No opportunity requiring satisfies, const type params, or using. Existing syntax is appropriate.

Per .anatoly/docs/03-Guides/02-Advanced-Configuration.md and .anatoly/docs/04-API-Reference/03-Types-and-Interfaces.md, wins containing WILDs must apply effectivePayout = basePayout × (1 + wildCount) × 2^wildCount. This function counts WILDs toward matchCount but never escalates for wild presence, producing lower payouts than the documented engine spec. Intentional for 'legacy' semantics but should be explicitly commented.
